We're waiting for them to get to the point. The point was the telling of the story. That's it, there is no point. And one of them is that is the point of their story. They, you know, and so also the obligation for, of course, because we're married, this is an unwelcome sentiment, however accurate it may be. So guys, it's best kept to yourself. And said in a different way. So really obligation in all of its forms exists but is not really motivating to anybody. So obligation is having to help a friend move. We do it, right? Because they're our friend, we have a truck, they don't, and they know we're not out of town, right? But nobody really is looking forward for two weeks to the experience of helping the friend move. That is actually how most people feel, most of the people you're selling to, it's how they feel about much of their business. They view it as obligatory. So if you ask most people why they get up in the morning and go to their job, so if you undoubtedly have somebody in your family, you probably have, everybody has fewer of them these days, but you probably have somebody in your family who has a job, brother, sister, son, daughter, you hope, son, daughter, you know somebody who has a job. If you ask them, what's the number one answer of why they get up and go to work? Pay the bills, number one answer. The way you get this poll information, by the way, is from Family Feud, okay? So Family Feud's a very educational show, if you don't watch it, you should watch it, because the whole game is based on a selection of options and who picks this one the most. So it's a public opinion poll turned into a game show is what it is. So the number one reason, and if you ask anybody, if you go take your own survey, why they get up in the morning and go to work at their job, the number one thing they say is to pay the bills. What's the number two thing? Because they'll get fired, okay? Number two reason is, because if I don't, I'll get fired, okay? Number one reason is to pay the bills. Now, if you ask small business owners why they get up in the morning and go to their business, what do you think they say? I love my job. Well, most of them are not honest. They give the answers they believe they should give. They give their obligatory answers. However, if you switch the question on them, so try this, if you switch the question on them and they don't understand the answer you're looking for, and you play this game.
